definition: to move closer to someone or something

sentences.text: We watched the train approach the platform.
She approached the problem carefully.
A stranger approached me in the street.

definition: to keep something in good condition or at the same level

sentences.text: It is important to maintain a healthy routine.
The company maintains high standards.
Regular service helps maintain the machine.

definition: to include someone or something as part of an activity

sentences.text: The project will involve several teams.
The job involves working with customers.
The change may involve some risk.

definition: to change your behaviour or ideas to suit a new situation

sentences.text: It can take time to adapt to a new city.
Businesses must adapt to change.
She adapted the plan to fit the budget.

definition: able to be trusted or depended on

sentences.text: We need a reliable source of information.
This car is old but reliable.
She is a reliable team member.

definition: working well without wasting time or resources

sentences.text: This new system is more efficient.
She found a more efficient way to work.
Public transport can be efficient in large cities.

definition: successful in producing the intended result

sentences.text: The new method was very effective.
Clear communication is an effective tool.
The medicine is effective against the infection.

definition: correct and free from mistakes

sentences.text: The report must be completely accurate.
The map gives accurate information.
Her prediction was surprisingly accurate.

definition: knowing about a situation or fact

sentences.text: Are you aware of the new rules?
Most people are aware of the problem.
She became aware that someone was following her.

definition: to accept something as true without proof

sentences.text: Do not assume that everyone agrees.
I assumed the meeting started at ten.
We should not assume the task is easy.

definition: to advise someone to do or choose something

sentences.text: I would recommend this book to beginners.
Doctors recommend regular exercise.
Can you recommend a good hotel?

definition: to recognise and name someone or something

sentences.text: Can you identify the main problem?
The police identified the suspect.
We need to identify areas for improvement.

definition: to stop something from happening

sentences.text: Regular checks can prevent accidents.
This lock prevents the door from opening.
Exercise may help prevent illness.

definition: to give full attention to something

sentences.text: Please focus on the main task.
She finds it hard to focus in noisy places.
The report focuses on customer needs.

definition: a particular way of doing something

sentences.text: This is a simple learning method.
We tested several methods.
The new method saves time.

definition: the outcome of an action or process

sentences.text: The final result was better than expected.
Hard work often produces good results.
We are waiting for the test results.

definition: facts or information that show something is true

sentences.text: There is no clear evidence to support the claim.
The police collected new evidence.
The results provide evidence of improvement.

definition: an answer to a problem

sentences.text: We need a practical solution.
They found a simple solution to the problem.
There is no perfect solution.

definition: the standard of how good something is

sentences.text: Customers expect high quality.
The product is known for its quality.
We need to improve service quality.

definition: a level of quality used for comparison

sentences.text: The product meets the required standard.
Safety standards are very strict.
Her work is always of a high standard.

definition: to roughly calculate or judge a value

sentences.text: Can you estimate the total cost?
We estimate that the work will take two days.
The engineer gave us a price estimate.

definition: to examine something carefully

sentences.text: We need to analyze the data first.
The team analyzed customer feedback.
Scientists analyze the results carefully.

definition: a plan designed to achieve a goal

sentences.text: We need a better marketing strategy.
The company changed its business strategy.
Planning is an important part of any strategy.

definition: to make someone admire or respect you

sentences.text: She wanted to impress the interviewer.
His presentation impressed the audience.
The restaurant failed to impress us.

definition: to make information publicly known

sentences.text: They will announce the winner tomorrow.
The company announced a new product.
She announced that she was leaving.

definition: to show or state that something is true

sentences.text: Please confirm your attendance by Friday.
The test confirmed our results.
He called to confirm the booking.

definition: to ask strongly for something

sentences.text: Customers demand better service.
The workers demanded higher pay.
This job demands a lot of patience.

definition: to meet or experience something unexpectedly

sentences.text: We may encounter some difficulties.
She encountered an old friend at the station.
The team encountered several technical problems.

definition: to pause before doing something because of uncertainty

sentences.text: Do not hesitate to ask for help.
She hesitated before answering.
He did not hesitate to accept the offer.

definition: to affect the way someone thinks or acts

sentences.text: Friends can influence our decisions.
Weather influences travel plans.
His teacher strongly influenced his career choice.

definition: to say firmly that something must happen

sentences.text: She continued to insist on paying.
He insisted that the report was correct.
They insist on high safety standards.

definition: to watch something carefully

sentences.text: Scientists observe the changes over time.
She observed how the machine worked.
We observed a clear improvement.

definition: to take part in an activity

sentences.text: Everyone can participate in the discussion.
She participated in the competition.
Many students participate in school activities.

definition: to carry out an action or task

sentences.text: The machine can perform the task automatically.
She performed well in the interview.
The doctor performed several tests.

definition: to show or express something

sentences.text: The results reflect the team's hard work.
His comments reflect his opinion.
Take time to reflect on your progress.

definition: to ask for something formally

sentences.text: You can request more information by email.
She requested a copy of the report.
The manager refused our request.

definition: something that is needed or demanded

sentences.text: Experience is a key job requirement.
The course has several entry requirements.
Safety training is a legal requirement.

definition: to choose something from a group

sentences.text: Please select an option from the menu.
The team selected the best candidate.
You can select multiple files.

definition: to be different in different situations

sentences.text: Prices vary depending on the season.
Results may vary from person to person.
The tasks vary in difficulty.

definition: to make a formal request or put something into use

sentences.text: I plan to apply for the position.
You can apply this method to other problems.
She applied for a visa online.

definition: to officially accept or agree to something

sentences.text: The manager must approve the plan.
The committee approved the budget.
Her parents did not approve of the decision.

definition: to express disagreement or give reasons for an opinion

sentences.text: They often argue about money.
She argued that the change was necessary.
It is difficult to argue with the evidence.

definition: to try to do something difficult

sentences.text: He will attempt to solve the problem.
She made another attempt to pass the exam.
Their first attempt was unsuccessful.

definition: a state in which different things have equal importance

sentences.text: It is hard to find a good work-life balance.
She balanced the box on one hand.
The budget must balance income and spending.

definition: to give all your attention to something

sentences.text: I cannot concentrate in a noisy room.
She concentrated on finishing the report.
Try to concentrate on one task at a time.

definition: to decide something after considering the facts

sentences.text: We can conclude that the plan worked.
The meeting concluded at six.
The report concludes with several recommendations.

definition: to give time, money, or effort to help achieve something

sentences.text: Everyone can contribute to the project.
Exercise contributes to good health.
She contributed several useful ideas.

definition: to persuade someone to believe or do something

sentences.text: Can you convince him to join us?
The evidence convinced me.
She convinced the manager to change the plan.

definition: to express disapproval of someone or something

sentences.text: It is easy to criticize without offering a solution.
The report criticized the company's policy.
She was criticized for arriving late.

definition: to show clearly how something works or is true

sentences.text: The teacher will demonstrate the process.
The results demonstrate clear progress.
He demonstrated how to use the equipment.

definition: to say what someone or something is like

sentences.text: Can you describe what happened?
She described the place in detail.
The report describes the current situation.

definition: to discover or decide something

sentences.text: We need to determine the cause of the problem.
The test will determine your level.
Several factors determine the final price.

definition: to create or set up something

sentences.text: They plan to establish a new company.
The study established a clear connection.
We need to establish some basic rules.

definition: to judge the quality or value of something

sentences.text: We should evaluate all the options.
The manager evaluated my performance.
Researchers are evaluating the results.

definition: to look at something carefully

sentences.text: The doctor will examine the patient.
We examined the data closely.
The report examines the effects of the change.

definition: to become or make something larger

sentences.text: The company plans to expand its services.
The business expanded into new markets.
Reading can expand your vocabulary.

definition: to show or communicate a thought or feeling

sentences.text: It is important to express your opinion clearly.
She expressed concern about the delay.
Art allows people to express themselves.
